line: GROVE_LESSONS:
- The repo already used `Dead` for process death, so the safest path was to extend that variant to also cover Dead UI rather than introducing a second severe state.

items: The repo already used `Dead` for process death, so the safest path was to extend that variant to also cover Dead UI rather than introducing a second severe state.

line: - Tracking “memory flat” needed one extra timestamp in state; comparing successive RSS/virtual samples was enough without changing persisted payload formats.

items: Tracking “memory flat” needed one extra timestamp in state; comparing successive RSS/virtual samples was enough without changing persisted payload formats.

line: GROVE_DECISIONS:
- Kept `AgentPhase::Dead` as the acceptance-criteria variant and broadened its semantics to mean “UI unresponsive or process gone.”

items: Kept `AgentPhase::Dead` as the acceptance-criteria variant and broadened its semantics to mean “UI unresponsive or process gone.”

line: - Dead stays higher priority than ApiHang, but only triggers after a stricter 300s threshold with CPU, token, JSONL, and memory-flat gating.

items: Dead stays higher priority than ApiHang, but only triggers after a stricter 300s threshold with CPU, token, JSONL, and memory-flat gating.

line: - ApiHang remains the 120s waiting-on-tool condition; Dead is intentionally not gated on `awaiting_tool_result` so it can catch UI wedged states.

items: ApiHang remains the 120s waiting-on-tool condition; Dead is intentionally not gated on `awaiting_tool_result` so it can catch UI wedged states.
